ip_outcome_note: Vision pass labeled suspected_ip as 'Graham (Final Fantasy Legends)' which is technically correct — 'Final Fantasy Legends' (FFL) is the Japanese-market alternate title for Final Fantasy Dimensions (2012 mobile RPG). The character Graham is from Final Fantasy Dimensions, not Final Fantasy Brave Exvius. Frontmatter updated: suspected_ip corrected from 'Graham (Final Fantasy Legends)' to 'Graham (Final Fantasy Dimensions)' for graph-precision; ip_verified set to true. This is a verify-with-clarification outcome: the character name is right, the game-brand label needed disambiguation.

trivia_bullets.text: IP clarification — 'Final Fantasy Legends' (FFL) is the Japanese-market alternate title for Final Fantasy Dimensions, a 2012 mobile RPG published by Square Enix. Graham is not from Final Fantasy Brave Exvius. The FFTCG card category abbreviation 'FFL' on Opus VIII cards refers to Final Fantasy Dimensions / Final Fantasy Legends. [finalfantasy.fandom.com/wiki/Final_Fantasy_Dimensions; WebSearch: Graham Final Fantasy Dimensions; WebSearch: Opus VIII FFL abbreviation]

Lore — Graham is a mysterious Dark Knight encountered by the Warriors of Darkness in Chapter 2 of Final Fantasy Dimensions. He inhabits the heavy plate armor of his fallen comrade Sigurd — slain by the fiend Baugauven — concealing that Sigurd is dead and that Graham himself was mortally wounded in the same battle, surviving as a spirit inhabiting the armor. After granting the Warriors of Darkness the Dark Knight job class, Graham abandons the armor and transforms into a cursed sword (Graham's Sword). His arc is defined by loyalty to a dead friend and quiet sacrifice. [finalfantasy.fandom.com/wiki/Graham; WebSearch: Graham Final Fantasy Dimensions Dark Knight chapter 2]
sources: WebSearch: Graham Final Fantasy Dimensions Dark Knight chapter 2

trivia_bullets.text: Set context — Opus VIII (March 22, 2019) listed 'FINAL FANTASY LEGENDS' as one of its featured game sources. The set's Dimensions / Legends representation is small relative to FFBE and FFXV, making Graham one of the rarer FFL-origin cards in the set. [fftcg.square-enix-games.com/na/release/opus-viii]

Mechanical design — Graham is a 5-cost Earth element Forward (9,000 Power, Dark Knight — FFL). His sole ability: when he is put from the field into the Break Zone, choose 1 Forward the opponent controls; if you have received 4 or more points of damage, break it. The death-trigger removal encodes his lore arc — Graham's value is realized at the moment of his removal, mirroring the narrative beat where his sacrifice grants the Warriors of Darkness the Dark Knight power. [WebSearch: FFTCG Graham 8-075C card text; WebSearch: Graham 8-075C cost power element]
